include_notes: Include only touched docs/static files and nearby style assets.
docs_static.context_reason: Docs/static work only needs the edited content and immediate style context.

include_notes: Include touched UI files, related CSS, and API contract stubs only if the UI depends on them.
ui.context_reason: UI work needs screen/component context plus the smallest API contract needed to avoid guessing.

include_notes: Include the relevant script or endpoint, config example, README or CLAUDE rules, and tests if present.
backend.context_reason: Backend/report/bot work needs the entry point, config shape, nearby tests, and project rules.

include_notes: Include only directly relevant auth, API, SQL, config docs, and tests.
sensitive.context_reason: Sensitive/security/live-prod work needs enough direct implementation context to be safe, with strict redaction.

include_notes: Include source docs, page references, extracted text, and affected generated files.
public_official.context_reason: Official public content needs source verification and affected output files, not broad repo context.

include_notes: Include bot script, CLAUDE.md, implementation notes, manifest if present, and relevant env-var docs.
live_forge.context_reason: Live Forge bot work needs the bot entry point and operational notes while excluding real environment values.
